======= RMM06367Suzanne Caygill papers6367Compiled by M. Warren, J. ParkerDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
May 20042B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Suzanne Caygill papers
Suzanne.
6367
22.2 cubic feet. 22.2 cubic feet.
1930-1990. Collection material in English Documentation concerning Caygill's Academy of Color. Video tapes, audio tapes, worksheets, scrapbooks, posters, samples, photographs, slides, and clippings. <<<<<<< HEAD 29 41 ======= 29 41 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 3544705
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Suzanne Caygill (1911-1994) was a milliner, dress designer and night club singer as a young adult. She turned her attention to color as early as 1940 and worked the rest of her life creating individual color palettes for clients and teaching design seminars. In the 1950s Suzanne Caygill starred in a self-improvement television program, "Living With Suzanne" which aired on CBS in Los Angeles. In 1980 she published Color, The Essence of You and established the Academy of Color. Michaelin Reamy (Watts), Cornell Class 1960, Suzanne's apprentice and professional assistant from 1988 until Ms. Caygill's death in 1994, arranged and coordinated the donation of the Suzanne Caygill Archives to Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Documentation concerning the Academy of Color including material for students, information about certification and accreditation, worksheets, and scrapbooks, posters, samples, photographs, slides, and clippings used in teaching. Also a set of 24 training video tapes from the Academy of Color including 19 tapes entitled "The Four Seasons"; 3 tapes entitled "Fabrics"; "The Key to Color Harmony," used to introduce clients to her theory; and "Living with Suzanne," excerpts from the television show, ca. 1952. Audio tapes of "The Key to Color Harmony," an abbreviated version of her theory; and "Poetry for Inspiration," in which Suzanne recites some of her favorite poems.
Typed mss. of Suzanne's book, Salon of Color business plan, interior design proposal and related letters, wardrobe design for Lisa Curry, schedule of events for May 1984, Academy of Color certificates, client comments, photograph, miscellaneous letters and schedules, and teaching manuals. Also, "Red Leather Seasons Portfolio" used by Suzanne in her teaching to show examples of the connection between a woman and her costume that fit a season, and various items of clothing, hats, and shoes worn by Suzanne.
Workbook created by Suzanne for Mary Cowies Moore of San Francisco, 1948.
Also, additional newspaper clippings about Suzanne and her work, original layout materials for her Salon of Color and course work, copies of her newsletter "Suzanne Says," and a 33 rpm phonograph record of her poetry readings. Also additional clothing, including a suit, blouse, evening dresses, hat, and a number of fabric evening stoles, some of which were designed by her.
Notebook compiled by Lisa Curry of handouts and class notes from Suzanne's seminars.
Conditions Governing Use
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions and Permissions page.
Conditions Governing Use
Audio visual media that has been digitized: V-8950 to V-8973, V-8975, V-11644 to V-11646, V-11648, V-11651, V-11653, V-11655 to V-11657, V-11659, V-11660, V-11662, V-11780 to V-11788, SR-6321, TR-10680, TR-10684, TR-10686 to TR-10753, TR-11644, TR-11646, TR-11648, TR-11651, TR-11653, TR-11655 to TR-11657, TR-11659 to TR-11660, TR-11662, TR-11780 to TR-11788
Cite As:
Suzanne Caygill papers, #6367. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials
Caygill's book Color, The Essence of You may be found here: https://hdl.handle.net/1813/55002
Series List
Series I. Files Boxes 1, 2, 5, 6, 17, 22, 23, 24, 30
Series II. Videos [Please only use media that has been digitized] Boxes 3
Series III. Sound recordings [Please only use media that has been digitized] Boxes 3, 4, 24
Series IV. Slides Box 4
Series V. Images Boxes 7-11, 16, 24, Mapcase Folder 1
Series VI. Fabric/clothing Boxes 12, 18-21, 25-28, Mapcase Folder 2
Series VII. RESTRICTED Boxes 13-15
Conditions Governing Use
Boxes 13-15 and 33-36 access is granted to researchers, but they cannot publish the names, addresses and phone numbers of clients that are listed on the cards.
Seasons. Color in interior decoration. Color in clothing. Color -- Social aspects -- History Fashion -- Forecasting Photographs Slides. Videotapes. Color Psychological aspects. Academy of Color

Biographical / Historical
Daniel and Eleanor Bell in slidesSalons of Color held in San Francisco1964-198032Series V. ImagesScrapbook pages, photographs, poem "Colors"n.d.7Images mounted on boardsn.d.Images mounted on boards8Images mounted on boards9Images mounted on boards102 framed images of SuzanneUndated11Red Leather Seasons portfolio16Photos, including Dolores Del Rio, Suzanne Caygill and "Master Class" group photo of women wearing hats designed by Caygilln.d.241Over-size Images on boards/collages (teaching materials)Undated1Negatives and photographs of winter seminar, fashions shows and unlabeled 1969Undated32Negatives of awards dinner198832Negatives of Suzanne teaching1950-195932Negatives unlabeledUndated32Salon of Color, Gore House and other unidentified photogrphsUndated32Small photo album of awards ceremony198832Large photo album1978-06-1978-0732
Scope and Contents
Includes photos of Suzanne Caygill; Tanglewilde, Wilderness, Vista Grove all in Washington; Wholistic Health Center in Puyallup Washington; Wholistice Health Center and condominum in Los Angeles and Jan Barrow, Don Turner, and Jim BuchnerSeries VI. Fabric/ClothingFabric samplesUndated12Clothing - including "Nothing Dresses" (silver and blue)Undated18Other clothing, shoes, jewelry and cosmetic casesUndated19HatsUndated20Tangerine angora wool dress, jacket, and shawl, designed by SuzanneUndated21Additional clothing25Additional clothing26Additional clothing27Additional clothing28Over-size fabric samples on boards (teaching materials)Undated2Coral/brown two piece outfit of dress and jacket. 193040
Scope and Contents
Outfit designed by Caygill in Chicago including the bead work.Series VII. Color Palette Cards and Fabric Swatches
Conditions Governing Use
Access is granted to researchers, but they cannot publish the names, addresses and phone numbers of clients that are listed on the cards.
Scope and Contents
These cards contain addresses and phone numbers of clients and occasionally mention the type of palette, eg. vital spring, but do not contain the actual color palette. They also contain billing information for consultation services.Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order13Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order14Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order15Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order1957-199033Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order1957-198934Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order1980-198938Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical orderUndated39Color palette fabric swatches created by Suzanne Caygill specifically for Julie Stockman196324 ======= Series I. Biographical / Historical

ImagesScrapbook pages, photographs, poem "Colors"n.d.7Images mounted on boardsn.d.Images mounted on boards8Images mounted on boards9Images mounted on boards102 framed images of SuzanneUndated11Red Leather Seasons portfolio16Photos, including Dolores Del Rio, Suzanne Caygill and "Master Class" group photo of women wearing hats designed by Caygilln.d.241Over-size Images on boards/collages (teaching materials)Undated1Negatives and photographs of winter seminar, fashions shows and unlabeled 1969Undated32Negatives of awards dinner198832Negatives of Suzanne teaching1950-195932Negatives unlabeledUndated32Salon of Color, Gore House and other unidentified photogrphsUndated32Small photo album of awards ceremony198832Large photo album1978-06-1978-0732
Scope and Contents
Includes photos of Suzanne Caygill; Tanglewilde, Wilderness, Vista Grove all in Washington; Wholistic Health Center in Puyallup Washington; Wholistice Health Center and condominum in Los Angeles and Jan Barrow, Don Turner, and Jim BuchnerSeries VI. Fabric/ClothingFabric samplesUndated12Clothing - including "Nothing Dresses" (silver and blue)Undated18Other clothing, shoes, jewelry and cosmetic casesUndated19HatsUndated20Tangerine angora wool dress, jacket, and shawl, designed by SuzanneUndated21Additional clothing25Additional clothing26Additional clothing27Additional clothing28Over-size fabric samples on boards (teaching materials)Undated2Coral/brown two piece outfit of dress and jacket. 193040
Scope and Contents
Outfit designed by Caygill in Chicago including the bead work.Series VII. Color Palette Cards and Fabric Swatches
Conditions Governing Use
Access is granted to researchers, but they cannot publish the names, addresses and phone numbers of clients that are listed on the cards.
Scope and Contents
These cards contain addresses and phone numbers of clients and occasionally mention the type of palette, eg. vital spring, but do not contain the actual color palette. They also contain billing information for consultation services.Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order13Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order14Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order15Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order1957-199033Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order1957-198934Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical order1980-198938Contact cards for clients seeking individual palettes in alphabetical orderUndated39Color palette fabric swatches created by Suzanne Caygill specifically for Julie Stockman196324 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
